title: ferray-io: NpzFile reads all entries into memory on open — no lazy loading
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NpzFile::open() reads every entry in the zip archive into memory as Vec<u8> entries. NumPy's NpzFile is lazy — it only reads array data when the specific array is accessed. For large .npz files with many arrays, ferray's eager loading wastes memory if only a few arrays are needed. Refactor to store the zip reader and only decompress/parse entries on demand (get method). This requires keeping the File/reader alive, which may need a self-referential struct or an inner Arc.
